rag_rules: Use assign only for implementation-level bindings of existing output features or auxiliary variables
Place output range guarantees in component type contracts when concrete bounds are available
Ensure AGREE annexes are properly closed with `annex agree {** ... **};`
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Remove invalid or unresolved AGREE clauses instead of adding architecture
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for boolean range expressions like `x >= min_value and x <= max_value`
Component type contracts should use assumptions and guarantees, while implementations use assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scope
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AADL property names as AGREE variables
